routes.postContent:
Mental Health Compliance in Louisville, KY

Mental health compliance in Louisville, KY at LMHG includes privacy, licensing, ethics, billing, records, and contact questions.

Reach Out
  1. Home
  2. FAQ
  3. Mental Health Compliance in Louisville, KY

Mental health compliance in Louisville, KY at LMHG includes privacy, licensing, ethics, billing, records, and contact questions.

HIPAA Compliance

We strictly adhere to the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) regulations. Your protected health information (PHI) is safeguarded through secure systems, confidential procedures, and staff training.

We do not disclose your information without your written consent, except as required by law or in emergency circumstances.

Privacy Practices

Your privacy is fundamental to the therapeutic relationship. We maintain confidential records and use secure communication methods. Our Notice of Privacy Practices describes how your information may be used and disclosed.

Licensing & Accreditation

Our clinicians are licensed professionals in the state of Kentucky. We maintain all required professional licenses and participate in ongoing professional development to meet licensure requirements.

Ethical Standards

We follow the ethical guidelines of our respective licensing boards and professional organizations. This includes maintaining appropriate professional boundaries, avoiding conflicts of interest, and prioritizing client welfare.

Insurance & Billing Compliance

We follow all applicable laws and regulations regarding insurance billing, including accurate coding and documentation. We work with most commercial insurance plans and Medicaid programs.

Questions About Compliance

If you have questions about our privacy practices, compliance standards, or your rights as a client, please contact us.

Contact Us

Phone

(502) 416-1416

Address

4229 Bardstown Rd, Suite 310, Louisville, KY 40218

More Ways To Compare Services

Use these pages to compare services, care settings, and practical next steps when you need more detail.

Services To Compare

Privacy Policy

Privacy policy for Louisville Mental Health Group website use, practice-related information, records, privacy limits, and contact questions.

View Page

Contact

Contact Louisville Mental Health Group about therapy, counseling, case management, community support, insurance, care settings, or starting care.

View Page

Questions About Compliance?

Contact the office if you have questions about privacy practices, compliance standards, records, or your rights as a client.

Reach Out

We focus on helping clients feel more stable, more clear, and more able to move forward. Home Mental Health Services Individual Therapy i
routes.postContent:
Individual Therapy in Louisville, KY

Individual counseling for adults and teens facing anxiety, depression, trauma, stress, relationship strain, or life changes.

Reach Out

What To Expect In Individual Therapy

Our individual counseling work is built around practical support, consistent care, and treatment that adapts to what is happening in your life right now. We focus on helping clients feel more stable, more clear, and more able to move forward.

  1. Home
  2. Mental Health Services
  3. Individual Therapy in Louisville, KY

We work with adults and adolescents across Louisville neighborhoods, Jefferson County, Bullitt County, and Oldham County, with telehealth and office-based options considered when appropriate.

Related Services And Specialties

Adult Counseling

Adult counseling for stress, anxiety, depression, grief, burnout, relationship strain, trauma, and major life changes.

View Service

Anxiety And Depression Therapy

Anxiety and depression therapy for worry, panic, sadness, low motivation, burnout, and emotional overwhelm.

View Service

Common Questions

Individual counseling commonly supports anxiety, depression, trauma, grief, stress, relationship strain, emotional overwhelm, and life transitions.

No. You can start by describing what feels hardest to manage, and the office can help identify whether individual counseling or another LMHG service is the right first step.

Call the office or use the intake form. You can share what is happening, ask about insurance, and talk through whether individual counseling is the right starting point.

Start With What Feels Hardest To Manage

Use the intake form or call (502) 416-1416 to ask about individual counseling, insurance, availability, and provider fit.

Reach Out

blocks.text: Individual counseling for adults and teens facing anxiety, depression, trauma, stress, relationship strain, or life changes.
